codeReview.summary: Fail. The fix restores only the previously connected subset of `active_servers`, so it still drops previously active failed servers when `drop_failed_servers` is false. That means strict `connect_all()` failures still do not preserve the full prior active server set required by the blocker and by VAL-MCP-001.

issues.description: `connect_all()` snapshots `previous_active_servers` by filtering `active_servers` through `connected_server_names`, so any previously active-but-failed servers are discarded before a later strict failure. With `drop_failed_servers = false`, the prior active set can intentionally include failed servers, but the strict error path now restores only the connected subset instead of the full previous active set that Python preserves.

summary: Reviewed the fix transcript skeleton, both feature diffs, the handoffs, the current `MCPServerManager` implementation, and the mission shared-state files. The new regression covers the default `drop_failed_servers=true` case, but the current fix is still incomplete because `connect_all()` restores only previously connected servers, not the full prior active set, so strict failures still regress when failed servers were intentionally kept active.
